findings.title: Implement Real-Time Co-Editing Feature
findings.description: Develop and implement a real-time co-editing feature that allows multiple users to edit documents simultaneously, addressing the critical need expressed by enterprise customers.
assumptions: Users will adopt the feature once implemented.
The infrastructure can support real-time collaboration.

evidence.excerpt: Competitor launched real-time co-editing — we need parity
Enterprise customers asking for simultaneous editing

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a document is open for editing WHEN multiple users access the document THEN all users can edit simultaneously without locking.

findings.title: Revise Document Locking Mechanism
findings.description: Redesign the document locking mechanism to allow for simultaneous editing, eliminating the current frustration users face with the locking model.
assumptions: Users prefer simultaneous editing over locking.
The new model will reduce support tickets.

evidence.excerpt: Current document locking causes user frustration

recommendations: Implement a new locking mechanism that supports real-time editing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a document is being edited WHEN another user attempts to edit THEN they can do so without being locked out.

findings.title: Add Presence Indicators
findings.description: Implement presence indicators to show who is currently viewing or editing a document, enhancing collaboration and reducing duplicate work.
assumptions: Users will find value in knowing who else is editing.
recommendations: Design and implement presence indicators for real-time collaboration.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN multiple users are editing a document WHEN a user accesses the document THEN they can see avatars of other users currently editing.

findings.title: Implement Conflict Resolution Strategy
findings.description: Develop a conflict resolution strategy for concurrent edits to prevent data loss and ensure user changes are preserved.
assumptions: Users expect changes to be saved without loss.
recommendations: Evaluate and implement a conflict resolution strategy.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN two users edit the same section of a document WHEN they save their changes THEN both changes are merged without data loss.

findings.title: Enhance Mobile Editing Experience
findings.description: Improve the mobile editing experience to support real-time collaboration, ensuring it is on par with desktop functionality.
assumptions: Mobile users require a seamless editing experience.
recommendations: Develop mobile features that support real-time collaboration.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is editing on mobile WHEN they access a collaborative document THEN they can edit seamlessly without locking issues.

findings.title: Collect User Feedback on Collaboration Features
findings.description: Establish a mechanism to gather user feedback specifically on the collaboration experience to inform future improvements.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for continuous improvement.

evidence.excerpt: Measurement Gap: User Feedback on Collaboration Experience

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user participates in collaboration sessions WHEN they complete a feedback form THEN their feedback is recorded and analyzed.
metadata.story_title: Collect User Feedback on Collaboration Features

findings.title: Monitor Daily Active Users Engaged in Collaboration
findings.description: Track the North Star metric of Daily Active Users engaged in collaboration to measure the success of the new features.
assumptions: Tracking DAU will provide insights into feature adoption.

evidence.excerpt: North Star Metric: Daily Active Users Engaged in Collaboration

recommendations: Set up monitoring for DAU metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the collaboration features are live WHEN users engage in collaboration sessions THEN DAU metrics are accurately tracked.
metadata.story_title: Monitor Daily Active Users Engaged in Collaboration

findings.title: Track Daily Collaboration Sessions
findings.description: Implement tracking for the primary KPI of daily collaboration sessions to assess user engagement with the real-time editing feature.
assumptions: Tracking collaboration sessions will help gauge feature effectiveness.
recommendations: Implement tracking for daily collaboration sessions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the collaboration features are live WHEN users initiate collaboration sessions THEN the number of sessions is accurately recorded.

findings.title: Monitor Blocked Concurrent Edit Attempts
findings.description: Track the guardrail metric of concurrent edit attempts blocked to ensure it does not increase as real-time collaboration features are implemented.
assumptions: Monitoring blocked attempts will help ensure the new features are effective.

evidence.excerpt: Guardrail Metric: Concurrent Edit Attempts Blocked

recommendations: Set up monitoring for blocked concurrent edit attempts.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the collaboration features are live WHEN users attempt to edit concurrently THEN the number of blocked attempts is accurately tracked.

findings.title: Conduct A/B Testing on Collaboration Features
findings.description: Implement A/B testing for different versions of real-time collaboration features to determine which version yields higher user engagement and satisfaction.
assumptions: A/B testing will provide valuable insights into user preferences.

evidence.excerpt: Experiment Opportunity: A/B Testing Collaboration Features

recommendations: Design and execute A/B tests for collaboration features.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN multiple versions of collaboration features are available WHEN users engage with them THEN A/B test results are collected and analyzed.
metadata.story_title: Conduct A/B Testing on Collaboration Features

summary: The analysis reveals a comprehensive set of requirements to enhance real-time collaboration features in CollabDocs, driven by critical customer insights and metrics. The findings include essential functionalities such as real-time co-editing, presence indicators, and conflict resolution strategies, alongside necessary tracking and feedback mechanisms to ensure ongoing improvement and user satisfaction.
